title: JavaScript Events Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with events and listeners.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript for responding to user clicks?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using the .addEventListener() method to listen for 'click' events on an element.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using .addEventListener() compared to using 'onclick' directly in your HTML?
options: It makes your HTML files smaller and more readable.
It allows you to add multiple listeners for the same event to one single element without overwriting them.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'e.preventDefault()' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
To stop the default browser behavior for an event (e.g., stopping a link from navigating so you can handle it with JS).
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' everywhere in your professional JavaScript events?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can make the site load much slower and potentially confuse the event handlers.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for JavaScript events?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using 'Event Delegation' to handle many similar events from one single parent element to improve performance.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
